The classification of this plant is further supported by its botanical name, which is a combination of the genus and species names. Arsenjevia flaccida is also known to be a rare and endangered species due to habitat loss and degradation.

intro: Arsenjevia flaccida, also known as Siberian golden grass, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Eastern Asia, characterized by its golden-yellow foliage and delicate, fragrant flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Arsenjevia flaccida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
